{"id":"project-LICHEN-ksz4","title":"UUID generation for sender_uid not deterministic - breaks message deduplication","description":"File: python/src/lichen/gateway/compact_cot.py
Lines: 346-347, 433

When expanding to XML, sender_uid defaults to a random UUID:
```python
if sender_uid is None:
    sender_uid = str(uuid.uuid4())  # Line 347
```

And chat messages generate a new random message_id:
```python
message_id = str(uuid.uuid4())  # Line 433
```

Impact:
1. Same compact CoT expanded multiple times produces different UIDs
2. ATAK/TAK servers may treat duplicates as distinct messages
3. No way to correlate mesh messages with their XML expansions
4. Deduplication at TAK server layer broken

The compact format should include a message identifier (or derive one deterministically from content hash + timestamp) to enable:
- Idempotent expansion
- Message correlation across systems
- Proper deduplication

Fix options:
1. Derive UID from hash of compact message bytes + timestamp
2. Add message sequence number to compact format
3. Document that caller MUST provide sender_uid for proper dedup","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:18:06.625313Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:18:06.62535Z"}
